name: A Hat in Time
description: A Hat in Time is a cute-as-peck 3D platformer featuring a little girl who stitches hats for wicked powers!

options.label: Progression Balancing
options.description: A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

options.label: Accessibility
options.description: Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

options.label: Endgoal
options.description: The end goal required to beat the game.

options.label: Actrandomizer
options.description: If enabled, shuffle the game's Acts between each other.

options.label: Shufflealpineziplines
options.description: If enabled, Alpine's zipline paths leading to the peaks will be locked behind items.

options.label: Finaleshuffle
options.description: If enabled, chapter finales will only be shuffled amongst each other in act shuffle.

options.label: Logicdifficulty
options.description: Choose the difficulty setting for logic.

options.label: Yarnbalancepercent
options.description: How much (in percentage) of the yarn in the pool that will be progression balanced.

options.label: Timepiecebalancepercent
options.description: How much (in percentage) of time pieces in the pool that will be progression balanced.

options.label: Randomizehatorder
options.description: Randomize the order that hats are stitched in.

options.label: Umbrellalogic
options.description: Makes Hat Kid's default punch attack do absolutely nothing, making the Umbrella much more relevant and useful

options.label: Startwithcompassbadge
options.description: If enabled, start with the Compass Badge.

options.label: Compassbadgemode
options.description: closest - Compass Badge points to the closest item regardless of classification important_only - Compass Badge points to progression/useful items only important_first - Compass Badge points to progression/useful items first, then it will point to junk items

options.label: Shufflestorybookpages
options.description: If enabled, each storybook page in the purple Time Rifts is an item check.

options.label: Shuffledirectortokens
options.description: If enabled, causes the Conductor/DJ Grooves tokens found in Chapter 2 levels to become item checks.

options.label: Shuffleactcontracts
options.description: If enabled, shuffle Snatcher's act contracts into the pool as items

options.label: Shufflesubconpaintings
options.description: If enabled, shuffle items into the pool that unlock Subcon Forest fire spirit paintings.

options.label: Nopaintingskips
options.description: If enabled, prevent Subcon fire wall skips from being in logic on higher difficulty settings.

options.label: Startingchapter
options.description: Determines which chapter you will be guaranteed to be able to enter at the beginning of the game.

options.label: Ctrlogic
options.description: Choose how you want to logically clear Cheating the Race.

options.label: Enabledlc1
options.description: Shuffle content from The Arctic Cruise (Chapter 6) into the game.

options.label: Tasksanity
options.description: If enabled, Ship Shape tasks will become checks.

options.label: Tasksanitytaskstep
options.description: How many tasks the player must complete in Tasksanity to send a check.

options.label: Tasksanitycheckcount
options.description: How many Tasksanity checks there will be in total.

options.label: Excludetour
options.description: Removes the Tour time rift from the game.

options.label: Shipshapecustomtaskgoal
options.description: Change the number of tasks required to complete Ship Shape.

options.label: Enabledeathwish
options.description: Shuffle Death Wish contracts into the game.

options.label: Dwshuffle
options.description: An alternative mode for Death Wish where each contract is unlocked one by one, in a random order.

options.label: Dwshufflecountmin
options.description: The minimum number of Death Wishes that can be in the Death Wish shuffle sequence.

options.label: Dwshufflecountmax
options.description: The maximum number of Death Wishes that can be in the Death Wish shuffle sequence.

options.label: Deathwishonly
options.description: An alternative gameplay mode that allows you to exclusively play Death Wish in a seed.

options.label: Dwenablebonus
options.description: In Death Wish, add a location for completing all of a DW contract's bonuses, in addition to the location for completing the DW contract normally.

options.label: Dwautocompletebonuses
options.description: If enabled, auto complete all bonus stamps after completing the main objective in a Death Wish.

options.label: Dwexcludeannoyingcontracts
options.description: Exclude Death Wish contracts from the pool that are particularly tedious or take a long time to reach/clear.

options.label: Dwexcludeannoyingbonuses
options.description: If Death Wish full completions are shuffled in, exclude tedious Death Wish full completions from the pool.

options.label: Dwexcludecandles
options.description: If enabled, exclude all candle Death Wishes.

options.label: Dwtimepiecerequirement
options.description: How many Time Pieces that will be required to unlock Death Wish.

options.label: Enabledlc2
options.description: Shuffle content from Nyakuza Metro (Chapter 7) into the game.

options.label: Baseballbat
options.description: Replace the Umbrella with the baseball bat from Nyakuza Metro.

options.label: Metrominponcost
options.description: The cheapest an item can be in any Nyakuza Metro shop.

options.label: Metromaxponcost
options.description: The most expensive an item can be in any Nyakuza Metro shop.

options.label: Nyakuzathugminshopitems
options.description: The smallest number of items that the thugs in Nyakuza Metro can have for sale.

options.label: Nyakuzathugmaxshopitems
options.description: The largest number of items that the thugs in Nyakuza Metro can have for sale.

options.label: Noticketskips
options.description: Prevent metro gate skips from being in logic on higher difficulties.

options.label: Lowestchaptercost
options.description: Value determining the lowest possible cost for a chapter.

options.label: Highestchaptercost
options.description: Value determining the highest possible cost for a chapter.

options.label: Chaptercostincrement
options.description: Lower values mean chapter costs increase slower.

options.label: Chaptercostmindifference
options.description: The minimum difference between chapter costs.

options.label: Maxextratimepieces
options.description: Maximum number of extra Time Pieces from the DLCs.

options.label: Finalchaptermincost
options.description: Minimum Time Pieces required to enter the final chapter.

options.label: Finalchaptermaxcost
options.description: Maximum Time Pieces required to enter the final chapter.

options.label: Yarncostmin
options.description: The minimum possible yarn needed to stitch a hat.

options.label: Yarncostmax
options.description: The maximum possible yarn needed to stitch a hat.

options.label: Yarnavailable
options.description: How much yarn is available to collect in the item pool.

options.label: Minextrayarn
options.description: The minimum number of extra yarn in the item pool.

options.label: Hatitems
options.description: Removes all yarn from the pool and turns the hats into individual items instead.

options.label: Minponcost
options.description: The minimum number of Pons that any item in the Badge Seller's shop can cost.

options.label: Maxponcost
options.description: The maximum number of Pons that any item in the Badge Seller's shop can cost.

options.label: Badgesellerminitems
options.description: The smallest number of items that the Badge Seller can have for sale.

options.label: Badgesellermaxitems
options.description: The largest number of items that the Badge Seller can have for sale.

options.label: Trapchance
options.description: The chance for any junk item in the pool to be replaced by a trap.

options.label: Babytrapweight
options.description: The weight of Baby Traps in the trap pool.

options.label: Lasertrapweight
options.description: The weight of Laser Traps in the trap pool.

options.label: Paradetrapweight
options.description: The weight of Parade Traps in the trap pool.

options.label: Death Link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies.

options.label: Death Link Amnesty
options.description: Amount of forgiven deaths before sending a Death Link.

options.label: Dw Death Link Amnesty
options.description: Amount of forgiven deaths before sending a Death Link during Death Wish levels.
